No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on 'optimal trade policy' and 'maximizing welfare of the society' provides an indirect link to creating policy frameworks for poverty reduction (Target 1.b).
evidence: affects the welfare of a country

Zero Hunger.reason: The course covers 'Instrument of trade policy' and 'economic principle of GATT and WTO,' which are directly relevant to correcting trade restrictions and distortions in world agricultural markets (Target 2.b).
evidence: economic principle of GATT and WTO

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The 'economic principle of GATT and WTO' can tenuously link to trade agreements concerning public health, such as the TRIPS Agreement and access to medicines (Target 3.b).
evidence: economic principle of GATT and WTO

Quality Education.reason: The course content does not directly address quality education, educational access, or skill development as defined by SDG 4 targets.

Gender Equality.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives links to clean water and sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives links to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly covers 'trade policy,' 'foreign direct investment,' and their impact on the 'welfare of a country,' which are central to sustaining economic growth (Target 8.1) and promoting productive activities (Target 8.3).
evidence: affects the welfare of a country

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on 'foreign direct investment,' 'structure of multinational firms,' and 'decisions of export' directly supports sustainable industrialization (Target 9.2) and enterprise integration into markets (Target 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's analysis of the 'economic principle of GATT and WTO' includes special treatment for developing countries (Target 10.a), and 'foreign direct investment' relates to financial flows to countries in need (Target 10.b).
evidence: economic principle of GATT and WTO

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives links to sustainable cities and communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course focuses on trade theory and policy, without explicit mention of sustainable consumption, production patterns, or resource efficiency as defined by SDG 12 targets.

Climate Action.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives links to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: The 'economic principle of GATT and WTO' can include discussions on fisheries subsidies negotiations, which are relevant to ending overfishing (Target 14.6).
evidence: economic principle of GATT and WTO

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives links to life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's analysis of the 'economic principle of GATT and WTO' directly relates to promoting the rule of law (Target 16.3) and strengthening participation in global governance (Target 16.8).
evidence: economic principle of GATT and WTO

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's core study of the 'economic principle of GATT and WTO' directly aligns with Target 17.10, which promotes a universal, rules-based multilateral trading system.
evidence: economic principle of GATT and WTO
